channel.title: Mini Diarium Blog
channel.description: Static articles about encrypted journals, private diary apps, offline journaling, and local-first software.

item.title: Journal App Without Cloud: What Actually Matters
item.description: A journal app without cloud dependency should keep entries local, work offline, encrypt writing before storage, and make export straightforward when you want full control.

item.title: Day One Alternative for Private Offline Journaling
item.description: Looking for a Day One alternative? The right replacement should keep journal entries local, work offline, support import and export, and make long-term ownership clearer.

item.title: Encrypted Journal App vs. Cloud Notes App
item.description: An encrypted journal app keeps private writing local and encrypted before storage, while a cloud notes app optimizes for sync and service convenience. The architecture changes who controls the words.

item.title: Offline Journal That You Own
item.description: An offline journal is not only about privacy. It also changes portability, continuity, and who controls the writing when the software or service around it changes.

item.title: Private Diary App for Desktop: What Actually Matters
item.description: A private diary app for desktop should work offline, encrypt entries at rest, keep storage local, and make exports available when you want your writing back.

item.title: Local-First Journaling Means You Keep the Exit Door Open
item.description: Local-first journaling is not only about privacy. It is also about durability, portability, and keeping your encrypted journal exportable when your tooling changes.

item.title: Why an Offline Journal Is Different From a Cloud Notes App
item.description: An encrypted offline journal changes who controls your writing, your risk surface, and your long-term ownership. Privacy-first journaling starts with architecture.

item.title: Why Mini Diarium Exists
item.description: Mini Diarium exists because private journaling software needed a maintained Mini Diary successor, rebuilt as an encrypted offline journal with a modern stack.
